channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Medical supplies in great need as international assistance flows into Myanmar after earthquake
item.description: Emergency aid has streamed into Southeast Asia in the two days since a massive earthquake struck Myanmar and Thailand.
item.dc:creator: Brian P. D. Hannon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600 and leaves countless buried
item.description: The smell of decaying bodies permeated the streets of Myanmar’s second-largest city on Sunday as people worked frantically by hand to clear rubble in the hope of finding someone still alive, two days after a massive earthquake struck that killed more than 1,600 people and left countless others burie
item.dc:creator: David Rising And Grant Peck, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Myanmar quake death toll rises to 1,644 as resistance movement announces partial ceasefire
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A unilateral partial ceasefire to facilitate earthquake relief efforts was announced on Saturday by Myanmar’s shadow National Unity Government, which coordinates the popular struggle against the ruling military. The country's death toll from the disaster soared to 1,644.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas says it accepts a new Gaza ceasefire proposal but Israel makes a counter-offer
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The Hamas militant group said Saturday it has accepted a new Gaza ceasefire proposal from mediators Egypt and Qatar, but Israel said it has made a counter-proposal in “full coordination” with the third mediator, the United States.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Countries sending humanitarian aid after Myanmar earthquake
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from Myanmar's powerful 7.7 magnitude earthquake keeps climbing amid rescue efforts. The military government said Saturday that 1,644 people have been killed, with thousands of others injured and dozens missing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump’s denial of Mexico’s Colorado River request sparks concerns over future water negotiations
item.description: The Trump administration’s unprecedented decision to deny a delivery of water to Mexico is raising alarm among experts, who fear it could jeopardize future cross-border negotiations in an increasingly thirsty region.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, The Hill

item.title: Doctor cites the pope's 'surprising improvement' after surviving life-threatening crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has shown ‘’a truly surprising improvement’’ since returning to the Vatican to convalesce after surviving a life-threatening bout with double-pneumonia, the doctor who coordinated the pontiff’s five-week hospitalization said Saturday. “I find him very lively,’’ Dr.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hundreds of thousands rally in Istanbul in support of imprisoned mayor
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of protesters again congregated in Istanbul Saturday to how their support for the city's imprisoned mayor and demand his release.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says military force not off the table for Greenland after Danish FM scolds his administration
item.description: The Danish foreign minister has scolded the Trump administration for its “tone” in criticizing Denmark and Greenland, saying his country is already investing more into Arctic security and remains open to more cooperation with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Philip Crowther, Kirsten Grieshaber And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UK bans ninja swords in move to crack down on violent knife crime
item.description: As knife crime continues to plague the U.K., the Labour government is attempting to crack down by adding ninja swords to the list of banned knives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's Signal scandal fuels growing distrust from allies
item.description: The Trump administration’s sharing of sensitive military information on Signal has shocked allies, while further eroding trans-Atlantic trust thanks to group chat messages from top U.S. officials expressing contempt toward Europe's “freeloading” on security.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: New US strikes against Houthi rebels kill at least 1 in Yemen
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pounded Yemen overnight into Saturday, reportedly killing at least one person as the American military acknowledged earlier bombing a major military site in the heart of Sanaa controlled by the Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli settlers seen on camera assaulting a Palestinian village. Police arrest only Palestinians
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Over a dozen Israeli settlers attacked a Palestinian village in the southern Israeli-occupied West Bank on Friday, beating residents with sticks and rocks, in an incident captured with rare clarity by security cameras.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel on Friday launched an attack on Lebanon's capital for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war in November.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian company seeks US permission to start deep-sea mining as outcry ensues
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — An abrupt announcement rattled members of a little-known U.N. agency based in Jamaica that has protected international deep-sea waters for more than 30 years. The Metals Company in Vancouver, Canada said late Thursday that it is seeking permission from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Countries that have issued travel advisories for US
item.description: Denmark, Finland, France, and Germany noted the U.S. requires individuals entering to choose male or female on ESTA and visa applications.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Myanmar at a glance: Embroiled in civil war, now facing more devastation after powerful earthquake
item.description: Even before a powerful 7.7-magnitude quake hit Myanmar on Friday, more than 3 million people in the country had been displaced, and hundreds of thousands were cut off from vital food and health programs as a result of a destructive four-year civil war that international groups claim has indiscrimina
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) warned in a speech Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline if they agree to a deal that achieves only “illusory peace.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Bali expands tourist guidelines over misbehavior from travelers
item.description: Rules include shelling out Bali's tourist levy — equivalent to $9 USD — and wearing "modest and respectful clothing" in public.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 150
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massive 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocks Thailand, Burma, collapsing buildings and killing more than 1,000
item.description: A massive 7.7 magnitude earthquake has caused buildings to collapse in Thailand and Burma, leaving more than 1,000 dead across both countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lithuanian, Polish troops aid in 'search and recovery' for 4 missing US soldiers from Fort Stewart
item.description: A Lithuanian official told Fox News Digital about ongoing search efforts to recover four missing U.S. Army soldiers in coordination with Poland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Human Rights Council blocks watchdog chief from criticizing UN official accused of antisemitism
item.description: The U.N. Human Rights Council blocked Hillel Neuer from finishing his remarks criticizing Special Rapporteur for the Occupied Palestinian Territories Francesca Albanese.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Signal leak: Trump adviser Mike Waltz may have to go, Warner says
item.description: “There needs to be accountability,” said Sen. Warner, who faults Trump's inner circle with a number of security gaffes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard, Ratcliffe, Patel criticized over Signal chat at hearing
item.description: Though the hearing is about the 2025 Annual Worldwide Threats Assessment, officials were questioned about a Signal group chat leak.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Amanda Knox reveals surprising relationship with man who prosecuted her
item.description: Amanda Knox, convicted, then acquitted of murder, discusses her time in an Italian prison and what freedom means to her now.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israel has struck the largest hospital in the territory's south
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel’s military struck the largest hospital in southern Gaza on Sunday night, killing two people, wounding others and causing a large fire, the territory's Health Ministry said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A Russian drone attack kills 7 in Ukraine ahead of ceasefire talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched a barrage of drones across Ukraine overnight Sunday that killed at least seven people, according to local Ukrainian officials and emergency services.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope returns to Vatican after 5-week hospital stay over life-threatening bout of pneumonia
item.description: A weak and frail Pope Francis returned home to the Vatican from the hospital on Sunday after surviving a five-week, life-threatening bout of pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
